name: Hedgpeth v. Pulido
question: Are jury instructions that instruct a jury on multiple theories of guilt, one of which is legally improper, necessarily "structural error" that exempts the instructions from a harmless-error review?
description: A case in which the Court held that the error of instructing a jury on multiple theories of guilt does not spoil all of the jury's findings, but rather the error should be evaluated by whether it caused a "substantial and injurious effect" on the verdict.
